response.justification: Wei Chen is an accomplished full-stack and systems software developer with strong web, mobile, and backend credentials, but has no demonstrated experience in any of the core competencies required for this role. There is no evidence of SoC or IP modeling, hardware-software co-design, AI accelerator performance analysis, GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD architectures, or deep learning workload optimization. The entire career trajectory is oriented toward web services, developer tooling, and GIS platforms — a fundamentally different domain than cloud infrastructure hardware optimization for AI.
strengths: 16+ years of continuous software development experience demonstrating strong engineering fundamentals and adaptability across languages and platforms
Prior experience with low-latency, high-throughput systems at Sociomantic (millions of HTTP requests/second) shows some performance engineering sensibility
Exposure to C/C++ and embedded-adjacent work (NI LabWindows, robotic arms, wave field synthesis thesis) provides a thin foundation in lower-level systems
concerns: Zero experience with SoC/IP modeling, hardware architecture, or any chip-level design — the core technical requirement of the role
No background in AI/ML frameworks, deep neural network training or inference, or GPU workload analysis — the primary application domain of the role
No evidence of hardware-software co-design, SIMD/MIMD architectures, or collaboration with silicon architects — all explicitly required or preferred qualifications
